title: Delivery-target handoff packages
description: Extend the engineering handoff export into explicit delivery-target packages. Add bounded export variants for common engineering workflows such as GitHub issue markdown, Jira-style ticket markdown/JSON, and Slack escalation message drafts. The goal is not to build live third-party integrations yet, but to make the exported case package immediately usable in real downstream systems.
done_when: the product can generate more than one delivery-target handoff format from the same incident packet
each delivery-target package preserves runtime-backed vs inferred-only evidence distinctions and mitigation context
the export surfaces feel like real downstream workflow packages rather than a single generic markdown blob

title: Replay-driven mitigation matrix
description: Deepen the remediation story by showing a bounded side-by-side mitigation matrix for the flagship incidents. The operator should be able to compare baseline, primary mitigation, fallback mitigation, and confidence deltas from replay-backed evidence instead of reading only one winning recommendation. Keep it grounded in the curated packs and measured outcomes where available.
done_when: the incident UI exposes a clear replay-driven mitigation matrix for the flagship outages
operators can compare at least baseline, primary mitigation, and fallback mitigation outcomes in one place
FORGE or GUARDIAN reasoning is visibly strengthened by the matrix instead of a single opaque recommendation

title: Runtime-host run queue and execution guardrails
description: Move runtime-host productization beyond capability visibility into bounded execution operations. Surface a lightweight run queue, current execution state, and guardrails such as replay eligibility, concurrency posture, and pack-specific runtime limits. The goal is to make runtime execution feel managed and trustworthy rather than hidden and instantaneous.
done_when: operators can see current runtime execution posture and queued or recent replay activity in-product
the product communicates execution guardrails and limits clearly for runtime-host use
runtime-host behavior feels operationally managed rather than opaque

title: Recurrence-aware outcome memory and reopen signals
description: Build on outcome-weighted memory by making recurrence visible. The product should surface when a current incident resembles a previously executed case that reappeared, when unresolved risk remained, and when an outcome should be treated as partial rather than final. This keeps the memory layer closer to real operational history instead of one-time wins.
done_when: memory can distinguish successful prior outcomes from recurring or partially-resolved cases
operators can see reopen or residual-risk signals when a similar incident reappears
the product’s memory story feels more operationally realistic and less optimistic-by-default

title: Curated pack validator and scaffold tooling
description: Turn the onboarding contract from item 51 into something enforceable. Add a validator and minimal scaffold path for new curated outage packs so the repo can check required metadata, replay hooks, debugger checkpoints, and coverage descriptors before a pack is considered supported.
done_when: the repo contains a bounded validator or scaffold path for new curated packs
missing required metadata or debugger/replay contract pieces are caught systematically
the product docs and code together make pack expansion feel disciplined instead of ad hoc

title: Approval audit timeline and governance export
description: Strengthen the enterprise trust layer by making GUARDIAN decisions and approval history more exportable and auditable. Show who approved what stage, what evidence posture existed at approval time, and what execution or outcome followed. Also provide a bounded exportable governance packet suitable for internal review or change records.
done_when: the product shows a clearer approval and execution timeline for a flagship incident
operators can export or inspect a bounded governance packet tied to evidence posture and outcome
the audit trail makes GUARDIAN feel more like a real governance surface

title: Final product-design polish and story sync
description: After the new engineering-delivery and governance surfaces land, do a final polish pass to keep the product coherent. This is not another random redesign; it is a story-sync pass so queue, incident, training, replay, exports, and docs all feel like one enterprise workflow product.
done_when: the product surfaces feel coherent after the new export, runtime-host, and audit additions
the story remains legible from intake through engineering handoff, approval, outcome, and memory
docs and UI language stay aligned without regressing the stronger visual identity from item 53
